identify_breaking_api.expected_solution: Agent must identify the breaking change in producer `grpc/grpc-go` introduced by grpc/grpc-go#2212. The IMPACT_REPORT.md must name the legacy symbols (transport.ClientTransport, transport.ServerTransport, transport.Stream, and transport.NewClientTransport) and their replacements ((none)), and point to producer-side artifacts (internal/transport/transport.go, internal/transport/http2_client.go, internal/transport/http2_server.go, internal/transport/handler_server.go, internal/transport/controlbuf.go, internal/transport/flowcontrol.go, internal/transport/http_util.go, clientconn.go, server.go, stream.go, and rpc_util.go).
evaluation_criteria: Names the producer repository `grpc/grpc-go` and the breaking PR/commit (grpc/grpc-go#2212)
Lists the deprecated/removed symbols: transport.ClientTransport, transport.ServerTransport, transport.Stream, and transport.NewClientTransport

References at least one producer-side file (basename: transport.go, http2_client.go, http2_server.go, handler_server.go, controlbuf.go, flowcontrol.go, http_util.go, clientconn.go, server.go, stream.go, and rpc_util.go)

find_direct_consumers.expected_solution: Agent must enumerate every consumer file in etcd-io/etcd and kubernetes/kubernetes that uses the deprecated symbols (transport.ClientTransport, transport.ServerTransport, transport.Stream, and transport.NewClientTransport). Per ground_truth: kubernetes: vendor/google.golang.org/grpc/clientconn.go, staging/src/k8s.io/apiserver/pkg/server/genericapiserver.go, pkg/kubelet/kubelet.go. The report must cite each consumer file by name and indicate which deprecated symbol it depends on.
evaluation_criteria: Cites each consumer file by name (basenames: clientconn.go, genericapiserver.go, and kubelet.go)
For each cited consumer, names which deprecated symbol from transport.ClientTransport, transport.ServerTransport, transport.Stream, and transport.NewClientTransport it uses
Distinguishes files with compile-time dependencies from files that only forward/proxy the symbol

trace_transitive_impact.expected_solution: Agent must trace transitive impact: which downstream code paths in etcd-io/etcd and kubernetes/kubernetes indirectly observe the producer change. The answer must identify forwarding/middleware/interceptor code that propagates the affected types and explain why the type or contract change cascades beyond the directly-importing files. Reference the consumer fix PR(s) (kubernetes/kubernetes#57160) where applicable.
evaluation_criteria: Identifies at least one transitive code path beyond the direct importers (interceptor/middleware/wrapper)
Explains the propagation mechanism (type changes, signature changes, behavior drift) for transport.ClientTransport, transport.ServerTransport, transport.Stream, and transport.NewClientTransport
Cross-references the consumer-side fix PR(s) (kubernetes/kubernetes#57160) or the migration pattern they encode

classify_breakage.expected_solution: Agent must classify each affected file by failure mode. The ground-truth breakage type is `compile_error`. For `compile_and_runtime` cases the report must identify both compile-time breakage (undefined functions, signature mismatches) and runtime behavior changes (silent metadata loss, semantic shifts). For `compile_only` or `runtime_only` cases, justify why only one class applies.
evaluation_criteria: Distinguishes compile-time failures (undefined functions, type errors) from runtime behavior changes (silent semantics drift)
Identifies at least one specific consumer file per failure class consistent with the classification
